topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The vase sat on the mahogany desk, a garish splash of pink and yellow against the muted grey of my workspace. I stared at the arrangement, my jaw tightening until a dull ache radiated behind my eyes. No note. Just a collection of overly cheerful roses and lilies, utterly devoid of explanation. I picked up a stem, the silk cool against my fingertips, and let it drop. It landed with a soft, irritating thud on the polished wood. "What is this supposed to mean?" I muttered to the empty room, the sound sharp and brittle. I immediately reached for the nearest paperweight, intending to flatten the entire display into oblivion. This gesture felt necessary; the sheer presumption of it all was grating on my nerves.

[story 2]

When the delivery man knocked, I didn't even look up from the spreadsheet spread across my desk. I heard the rustle of cellophane and the faint, saccharine scent of cut petals before I finally acknowledged the intrusion. The bouquet was undeniably opulent, a ridiculous display of sentimentality. I let my gaze drift over the vibrant blooms, my lips pressing into a thin, unyielding line. I took the arrangement with two fingers, treating it as if it were contaminated. "Just leave it," I managed, my voice clipped and devoid of warmth. I tucked the flowers into the corner of my filing cabinet, a silent declaration that whatever saccharine gesture this was, it held absolutely no weight in my world.

[story 3]

The moment the box was placed on the entryway table, a cold knot formed in my stomach. I watched from the doorway as someone—someone I vaguely recognized, someone whose presence I actively wished to ignore—picked up the flowers. They were too bright, too earnest. I crossed my arms tightly across my chest, a deliberate barrier against any perceived intrusion. My breathing hitched slightly, a small, involuntary reaction to the sheer audacity of the unsolicited gift. When they finally turned to leave, I made sure my expression remained perfectly neutral, my eyes fixed on a distant point on the wall, conveying a profound sense of being utterly unimpressed by the entire exchange.
